Valley – beesayatv https://beesayatv.com/ Documenting Cebu trails, one quiet hike at a time. Fri, 24 Jul 2026 05:00:31 +0000 en-US hourly 1 https://wordpress.org/?v=7.0.2 https://beesayatv.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/cropped-09-32x32.png Valley – beesayatv https://beesayatv.com/ 32 32 Camp 8 to Matun-og Falls Hike (Anxiety Took Over) | Camp 8, Minglanilla | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/camp-8-to-matun-og-falls-hike-anxiety-took-over-camp-8-minglanilla-2026-gpx/ Sat, 04 Jul 2026 10:38:04 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=487 Camp 8 to Matun-og Falls is a documented out-and-back community trail in Minglanilla. The route descends into a valley through farms, mountain viewpoints, and local settlements before continuing toward Matun-og Falls. Although the distance is relatively short at approximately 6.2 km, the continuous climbs and descents require good endurance and careful navigation.

Trail Snapshot

TOUGH
DocumentedJuly 2026
Distance6.2 km (Out and Back)
Elevation Gain457 m
Duration4-5 hrs
WaterAt least 2 liters
ShadeLimited
Surface100% trail
GPXWikiloc

Forecast data: Open-Meteo

Access

The documented hike began from Camp 8, Minglanilla via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the road near the jump-off point, with no parking fee observed during the hike. Private vehicles were able to access the trailhead, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.

Why this trail is rated Tough

✓ Significant endurance required
✓ Sustained steep ascent or descent
✓ Limited or no mobile signal
✓ Navigation requires attention
✓ Limited bailout options

On this trail: farmland • river crossing • valley

Route Description

The route follows a mostly straightforward community trail with steady climbs and descents through farmland, open mountain scenery, and local settlements. Several sections provide mountain viewpoints, while frequent encounters with local residents are common along the way.

Although the trail is not isolated, some portions feel more secluded because of dense vegetation. Navigation requires attention, as multiple paths branch off toward different houses and destinations.

The documented hike did not reach Matun-og Falls. The hike was turned back before the destination due to bad weather, dense vegetation, and a river crossing. Although there was no heavy downpour, the decision to return was made as a precaution.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were cloudy with occasional rain. The trail was dry at the time but was observed to become very muddy and slippery after rainfall. Shade coverage was limited, and no stores were encountered along the route. Mobile signal was strong near the start before disappearing farther into the trail.

At least 2 liters of water was recommended for this hike. Many insects were present, and the only people encountered during the hike were local residents.

Additional Notes

A trekking pole or walking stick is highly recommended because of the continuous ascents and descents. It may also be useful when encountering dogs along the route.

Farm animals, including carabaos and cows, were commonly encountered. When necessary, briefly leaving the GPX track to maintain a safe distance while passing was noted as a practical option.

]]> Toong Lumadnon Trail | Pardo / Buhisan | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/toong-lumadnon-trail-pardo-buhisan-2026-gpx/ Wed, 01 Jul 2026 06:51:39 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=412 The Toong Lumadnong Trail is a loop hike connecting the trails of Toong, Dulhogan, and Buhisan. The route passes through farmland, shaded sections, and the Buhisan Tree Tunnel before returning to the starting point. The hike is generally non-technical, with gradual elevation changes and a mix of open and shaded terrain.

Trail Snapshot

MILD
DocumentedApril 2026
Distance7 km (Loop)
Elevation Gain320 m
Duration3 - 3.5 hrs
WaterAt least 2 liters
ShadeLimited
Surface85% trail, 15% road
GPXWikiloc

Forecast data: Open-Meteo

Access

The documented hike began near Toong Barangay Hall, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the jump-off point, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.

Why this trail is rated Mild

✓ Easy-to-follow trail
✓ No technical climbing required
✓ Moderate physical effort required
✓ Sustained moderate ascent or descent
✓ Long exposed sections with little shade

On this trail: sunrise view • sunset view • valley

Route Description

The route begins with a mostly uphill section through farmland before continuing along a combination of open trails and shaded areas. The trail passes Lumadnong Pool, Dulhogan, and the Buhisan Tree Tunnel as it progresses around the loop.

After the initial ascent, the route transitions into a long downhill return toward the starting point. Although the trail is not technical and does not include sustained steep climbs, several sections are exposed to the sun.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. Shade coverage varied between open farmland and shaded sections. No stores were encountered along the route.

Mobile signal was strong near the start but gradually weakened farther into the trail. At least 2 liters of water was recommended. Few insects were observed, and only minimal foot traffic was encountered during the hike.

Additional Notes

Some sections of the route may feel isolated, although scattered houses are present along the trail.

]]> Camp 7 Farm Trails | Minglanilla Part 2 | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/camp-7-farm-trails/ Fri, 26 Jun 2026 03:46:21 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=264 Farm Trails is a 10 km hike connecting the back trails of Camp 7, Lubas, Ylaya, and Camp 8 in Minglanilla. The route passes through a man-made forest, farmland, and rolling countryside, following a series of gradual climbs and descents across mostly open terrain. While the trail is generally easy to follow, the distance and repeated elevation changes require moderate physical effort.

Trail Snapshot

MILD
DocumentedMay 2026
Distance10 km (Loop)
Elevation Gain337 m
Duration4-5 hrs
WaterAt least 2 liters
ShadeLimited
Surface75% trail, 25% road
GPXWikiloc

Forecast data: Open-Meteo

Access

The documented hike began from a roadside jump-off reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the starting point, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.

Why this trail is rated Mild

✓ Mostly easy-to-follow trail
✓ Longer hiking distance
✓ Sustained moderate ascent and descent
✓ Moderate physical effort required
✓ Long exposed sections with little shade

On this trail: cliff • farmland • ridge • roads • valley • viewpoint

Route Description

The hike begins in a man-made forest before continuing onto farmland trails and open countryside. The route follows repeated gradual climbs and descents through rolling terrain, with long views of the surrounding hills along several sections.

As the hike progresses, the accumulated elevation gain may become more noticeable, particularly later in the route. The return includes extended downhill sections. Most of the trail is open, with very little shade available.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. The trail was dry at the time but can become muddy during rainy weather. Shade coverage was limited throughout most of the route, and no stores were encountered along the trail.

Mobile signal was strong near the trailhead before becoming weak or disappearing farther along the route. At least 2 liters of water was recommended. Few insects and low foot traffic were observed during the hike.

Additional Notes

The route begins with a section through a man-made forest, providing a gradual warm-up before reaching the more open farmland trails.

These are farmland trails with local activity, and the route is not considered isolated.

]]> Toong Forest Trail | Toong, Pardo / Mahayahay | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/toong-forest-trail/ Fri, 26 Jun 2026 03:37:28 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=260 Toong Forest Trail Hike is a short hike connecting the trails of Toong, Mahayahay, and Buhisan. The route passes through farmland, open hillsides, and a small mountain community before descending toward Kawa Kawa Falls. The trail is easy to follow, with gradual elevation changes and low route commitment.

Trail Snapshot

EASY
DocumentedMay 2026
Distance5.08 km (Point-to-Point)
Elevation Gain211 m
Duration2-3 hrs
WaterAt least 1 liter
ShadeLimited
Surface60% trail, 40% road
GPXWikiloc

Forecast data: Open-Meteo

Access

The documented hike began near Toong Barangay Hall, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the jump-off point, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.

For the documented hike, the motorcycle was left near populated areas for added security and as part of the warm-up walk.

Why this trail is rated Easy

✓ Easy-to-follow trail
✓ Navigation is straightforward
✓ No technical climbing required
✓ Route commitment is low
✓ Short hiking distance

On this trail: farmland • river crossing • valley

Route Description

The route begins with gradual uphill sections through rolling farmland before continuing across mostly open terrain. Around 2.3 km into the hike, the trail passes through a small mountain community after a steep uphill climb.

Beyond the community, the route continues toward Kawa Kawa Falls, which was reached at approximately the 5 km mark. The trail then descends toward the falls, with some steeper downhill sections that may be tiring on the knees.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. The trail was dry at the time but can become muddy during rainy weather. Most sections were slightly exposed, with no stores encountered along the route.

Mobile signal was strong near the trailhead before becoming weak or disappearing farther along the route. At least 2 liters of water was recommended. Few insects and low foot traffic were observed during the hike.

Additional Notes

The return from Kawa Kawa Falls was not included in the GPX recording. Part of the return route followed a long climb on a concrete road.

]]> Camp 8 Backtrails | Minglanilla Part 3 | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/ylaya-camp-8/ Thu, 25 Jun 2026 13:47:11 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=236 Camp 8 Backtrails is a short out-and-back hike through the farmland trails of Ylaya in Camp 8, Minglanilla. The route follows rolling countryside with a gradual climb to its highest point before returning along the same trail. The moderate elevation gain and straightforward navigation make it suitable for hikers looking for a short mountain walk without demanding ascents.

Trail Snapshot

EASY
DocumentedMay 2026
Distance6.1 km (Loop)
Elevation Gain220 m
Duration2.5 - 3 hrs
WaterAt least 2 liters
ShadeModerate
Surface80% trail, 20% road
GPXWikiloc

Forecast data: Open-Meteo

Access

The documented hike began from a roadside jump-off in Ylaya, Camp 8, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the starting point, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.

Why this trail is rated Easy

✓ Easy-to-follow trail
✓ Navigation is straightforward
✓ No technical climbing required
✓ Route commitment is low
✓ Short hiking distance

On this trail: farmland • valley • viewpoint

Route Description

The route begins with a gradual climb through farmland and rolling hills, with the elevation gain spread steadily across the trail rather than concentrated in steep sections. The hike continues to the highest point before returning along the same countryside paths.

The return is generally easier, although a few rolling sections remain along the route. The trail is non-technical and follows a straightforward path throughout.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. The trail was dry at the time but can become muddy after rain. The opening section was shaded, while most of the route was exposed. No stores were encountered along the trail.

Mobile signal was strong near the trailhead before becoming weak or disappearing farther along the route. At least 2 liters of water was recommended. Few insects and low foot traffic were observed during the hike.

Additional Notes

This route forms a small segment of the larger Minglanilla Trail Run 2026 route.

The trail passes through farmland and is not considered isolated.

]]> Bitlang Trail Loop | Sudlon 1, Cebu City | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/bitlang-trail-loop/ Thu, 25 Jun 2026 10:03:27 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=205 Bitlang Trail Loop is a documented hiking route in the uplands of Sudlon 1, Cebu City. The route descends via Bitlang Trail toward Bonbon before following the road to Buot-Taup. From there, the hike returns to Sitio Tabla through a sustained uphill climb, completing a loop of approximately 12.9 km. While the descent is generally straightforward, the prolonged ascent from Buot-Taup is the defining challenge of the route and accounts for its Tough rating.

Trail Snapshot

TOUGH
DocumentedJune 2026
Distance12.89 km (Loop)
Elevation Gain602 m
Duration6-7 hrs
WaterAt least 3 liters
ShadeModerate
Surface60% trail, 40% road
GPXWikiloc

Forecast data: Open-Meteo

Access

The documented hike began near the covered court in Sitio Tabla, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the jump-off point. V-hires were available along the Transcentral Highway route, and habal-habal services were also available.

Why this trail is rated Tough

✓ Long hiking distance requiring sustained endurance
✓ Long or sustained climbs and descents
✓ Prolonged physical effort over several hours
✓ High route commitment with limited bailout options
✓ Assistance or evacuation may take considerable time

On this trail: farmland • forest • ridge • valley • viewpoint

Route Description

The route begins in Sitio Tabla before descending through narrow trails, farmland, small settlements, and river sections toward Bonbon. After leaving the trail, the route follows the local road to Buot-Taup, where the character of the hike changes. From Buot-Taup, a sustained uphill climb returns to Sitio Tabla, completing the documented loop. Although the descent is generally straightforward, the prolonged ascent requires significantly greater endurance and is the defining challenge of the hike.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were cloudy throughout the day. Rain the night before left portions of the trail slightly muddy. The route alternated between shaded forest sections and more exposed farmland and road segments. Stores were available near local communities before and during the hike.

Mobile signal was strong near the start but became unavailable in parts of the route. Hikers completing the full loop should carry at least 3 liters of water, particularly during warmer weather when the prolonged ascent from Buot-Taup can significantly increase physical exertion. No other hikers were encountered during the documented hike, while mosquitoes were present in sections with dense vegetation.

Additional Notes

This report documents the complete Bitlang Trail Loop. The accompanying video, however, covers only the approximately 5 km descending section of Bitlang Trail. The return from Buot-Taup to Sitio Tabla involves a sustained uphill climb of approximately 4 km and is not shown in the video.

Stores are available between the Bitlang Trail exit and the beginning of the return ascent. On sunny days, an umbrella may be helpful, as much of the route is exposed with limited tree cover.

The route has no dangerous cliffs or exposed ridges. A walking stick or trekking pole may help improve balance on uneven terrain and reduce strain on the knees, particularly during the prolonged return climb.

]]>
